stories.title: 2026 CNBC Disruptor 50 list: Why Anthropic was No. 1 in this year's rankings
stories.summary: Anthropic, posting explosive growth for its powerful AI systems that enterprises trust, has leapfrogged OpenAI on the CNBC Disruptor 50 list as the new No. 1.

stories.title: Trump claims Xi will not arm Iran as strike threat looms
stories.summary: US president warns Tehran has ‘two or three days’ to reach a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Anthropic hires OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy, former Tesla AI leader
stories.summary: Andrej Karpathy, an artificial intelligence researcher who co-founded OpenAI before getting poached by Tesla, said he's joining Anthropic.

stories.title: US Consumer Navigating Shocks 'Remarkably Well,' Says Michelle Meyer
stories.summary: Elevated energy prices have stoked inflation fears, raising the risk that the Federal Reserve and other central banks may need to keep rates elevated. Michelle Meyer, Chief Economist and Head of the Economics Institute at Mastercard, discusses how the consumer is faring amid an increasingly uncertain economic picture, especially as summer travel picks up. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Williams CEO Sees Gas Demand Outpacing Past Years on Powering AI
stories.summary: Demand for US natural gas is set to rise in the next 10 years more than it did for the past 15 years amid growing power needs from data centers supporting artificial intelligence and a global push for energy security, according to the head of Williams Cos.

stories.title: US Sanctions 50 Tehran-Linked Entities in Iran, UAE and China
stories.summary: The US sanctioned over 50 entities, including oil and gas tankers as well as an Iranian foreign currency exchange, in its continued bid to get the Islamic Republic to agree to a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Putin and Xi to discuss huge gas project at China summit
stories.summary: Talks on Power of Siberia 2 pipeline come just days after Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing

stories.title: Bessent urges G7 to help U.S. attack Iran's finances
stories.summary: The Iran war continues to wreak havoc on global oil supply and the broader economy, even as the conflict appears to be locked in an uneasy stalemate.

stories.title: OpenAI Founding Member Andrej Karpathy Takes Role at Anthropic
stories.summary: Andrej Karpathy, a founding member of OpenAI who later went to work for Tesla Inc., has joined Anthropic PBC to do research and development focused on helping train new artificial intelligence models.

stories.title: Bessent Urges Allies to Aggressively Enforce Iran Sanctions
stories.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent pressed US allies to join the US in enforcing sanctions on Iran and other malicious actors, saying, “Crushing the threat of terrorism compels all of you to step up and join us in rooting out the financing that sustains it.” Bessent addressed the No Money for Terror conference on Tuesday, part of the meeting of the Group of Seven Nations in Paris. (Source: Bloomberg

stories.title: Behind the AI Debt Boom
stories.summary: JPMorgan’s David De Boltz explains why Wall Street is pouring unprecedented money into AI infrastructure, data centers, and GPU financing, while abandoning parts of the software market. On Bloomberg Open Interest, he breaks down the $5 trillion AI buildout, why investors are still buying despite rising rates, and the hidden refinancing risks emerging in tech credit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump Denies Xi Told Him That Putin Would Regret Ukraine War
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump denied reports that his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping, offered a bleak assessment of Russia’s war in Ukraine, including claiming that Vladimir Putin would regret invading his neighbor.

stories.title: Fed’s Perli Says T-Bill Purchases Can Be Adjusted Up or Down
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of New York’s Roberto Perli said the central bank’s monthly Treasury bill purchases are not on a pre-determined course and can be adjusted depending on market conditions.

stories.title: How Natural Gas Will Power the AI Future
stories.summary: Williams CEO Chad Zamarin joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the AI boom is triggering the biggest natural gas demand surge in decades. He explores why data centers are overwhelming the power grid, why natural gas is becoming the backbone of AI infrastructure, and how America’s energy “superpower” could protect the US from global energy shocks. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Putin Arrives in China for Xi Talks With Gas Project on Agenda
stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin arrived in Beijing late Tuesday, aiming to reinforce ties with Chinese leader Xi Jinping and make progress on a long-stalled energy project.

stories.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent exhorted US allies to join the US in “aggressively” enforcing sanctions on Iran and other malicious actors and said there was “no room for excuses” for those who wish to crack down on terror financing.

stories.title: Russia Conducts Massive Nuclear Drills as Putin Visits China
stories.summary: Russia is holding a major combat exercise to practice preparing and using its nuclear forces at the same time as President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

stories.title: US 30-Year Yield Hits Highest Since 2007 on Inflation Angst
stories.summary: Yields on the US Treasury’s longest-dated bond rose to the highest level in almost two decades as investor concern over accelerating inflation fuels a selloff across global debt markets.

stories.summary: Global bond markets remain on edge as traders monitor central bank responses to renewed inflation fears.

stories.title: Mortgage rates surge to highest level since July
stories.summary: Mortgage rates continued to move higher Tuesday on uncertainty over the war with Iran. They are at the highest level since last July.

stories.title: Trump Team Seeks Longer Gas Tax Holiday With Phased-In Return
stories.summary: The Trump administration is working with Congress to suspend federal excise taxes on gasoline and diesel as the war in Iran drives up oil and fuel costs.

stories.summary: India’s Bharat Petroleum Corp. Ltd. has largely shifted to making spot crude purchases as the Iran war upends annual arrangements for Middle Eastern barrels, highlighting challenges for refiners to secure supplies.

stories.title: Burgum Dismisses US Oil Export Curbs as ‘Bad on All Accounts’

stories.summary: At the European arm of failed crypto exchange FTX, Patrick Gruhn watched as traders repeatedly lost money on risky bets. Now, he’s launching a product where the savviest investors can turn a profit — even while putting zero capital at stake.

The global investment bank has been a key player in the region with a history of advising Gulf transactions including the Aramco IPO. stories.summary: China is seeking to raise as much as 6 billion yuan ($882 million) in a green sovereign bond sale in Hong Kong next week, as the nation continues to build its presence in offshore debt markets and aims to fund climate action.

stories.title: StanChart CEO Winters Says AI to Replace "Lower-Value Human Capital"
stories.summary: “It’s not cost cutting; it’s replacing in some cases lower-value human capital.” Standard Chartered CEO Bill Winters delivered a blunt message on the future of the bank’s workforce.
